title: NVIDIA Tools Profile v1
description: Canonical annotation schema for ATOF scope_type='tool' events: describes the shape of annotated_request / annotated_response when the schema identifier is 'nvidia/tools.v1'. Captures every field needed to populate a complete ATIF ToolCall (tool_call_id + function_name + arguments) plus tool-execution metadata (provider, type, status, latency, retries, parent linkage) — making a tool ScopeEndEvent self-describing so consumers can build the ATIF ToolCall from the tool event alone, without joining against the upstream LLM event. All fields are optional. See atof-schema-profiles.md §5 (Canonical Schema Registry) + §6 (Schema Resolution Protocol). Schema ID format: vendor/<id>.v<N> per atof-schema-profiles.md §3.1.

tool_call_id.description: Correlation ID from the LLM tool-call response — e.g., 'call_abc123' (OpenAI) or 'toolu_abc123' (Anthropic). Mirrors the ATOF event's 'profile.tool_call_id' (spec §4.4). Maps directly to ATIF ToolCall.tool_call_id and observation.results[*].source_call_id.

function_name.description: The function or tool name being invoked — e.g., 'calculator__add', 'web_search', 'mcp.filesystem.read_file'. Maps directly to ATIF ToolCall.function_name. Duplicates info available in the LLM ScopeEnd's tool_calls list, but lets the tool event stand alone for streams where the LLM event is in a different session, was not captured, or for non-LLM-triggered tools (scheduled tasks, agentic loops).

arguments.description: Arguments dict passed to the function. Maps directly to ATIF ToolCall.arguments. When the upstream LLM emitted arguments as a JSON string, the producer SHOULD parse it to a dict here; if parsing fails, emit {'_raw': '<original-string>'}.

tool_type.description: Category of the tool. 'function' is the OpenAI-style schema-bound function call. 'mcp' is a Model Context Protocol server tool. 'custom' is a vendor-specific runtime extension.

tool_provider.description: Provider/registry that supplied the tool — e.g., 'langchain', 'llama_index', 'crewai', 'mcp:filesystem', 'mcp:postgres', 'native', 'nvidia.guardrail'. For MCP servers use the 'mcp:<server_name>' convention.

parent_tool_call_id.description: tool_call_id of the parent tool when this is a nested-tool invocation (e.g., a tool that internally calls another tool). Null at the top level. Lets consumers reconstruct the tool call graph without walking parent_uuid chains.

execution.description: Runtime execution metadata captured at scope end. Distinct from ATOF's own ScopeEndEvent.status (which is the lifecycle outcome) — these fields carry richer detail for retry tracking, cache hits, and sub-status semantics.

status.description: Detailed execution outcome. SHOULD be consistent with ATOF ScopeEndEvent.status: 'succeeded'/'succeeded_with_retries'/'cached'/'fallback' map to status='ok'; 'failed'/'timeout' map to 'error'; 'cancelled' maps to 'cancelled'.

error_type.description: Exception class or error category — e.g., 'TimeoutError', 'PermissionDenied', 'RateLimitExceeded'. Surface for filtering and aggregation; the human-readable message lives in ATOF ScopeEndEvent.error.message.

retries.description: Number of retry attempts made before this final result. 0 = succeeded on first try.

latency_ms.description: End-to-end execution latency in milliseconds (request to result). Convenience surface — derivable from ATOF Start/End ts_micros.

cached.description: Whether the result was served from a cache without re-executing the underlying tool.

cache_key.description: Cache key used or computed for this invocation. Useful for debugging cache hit/miss patterns.

input_size_bytes.description: Serialized size of the arguments payload in bytes. Useful for tool throughput and cost analysis without re-serializing 'arguments'.

output_size_bytes.description: Serialized size of the tool's return value (which lives in ATOF ScopeEndEvent.output) in bytes.

provider_metadata.description: Vendor-specific metadata bag — provider-defined keys that don't fit the canonical schema. Examples: MCP server response code, langchain run-id, web_search query expansion details, code_interpreter sandbox ID. Keep small (< 1 KB recommended); large blobs belong in ATOF data/metadata fields.
